info.title: NousViz API
info.description:
**NousViz API** — operator-facing platform API.

## Authentication

Most endpoints require a session token in the `X-Session-Token` header. Get
one via `POST /api/auth/login`. Some endpoints accept an API key via
`X-API-Key` instead.

## Where to find what

- [/docs/api](/docs/api) — interactive native reference (operators)
- [/openapi.json](/openapi.json) — raw spec, JSON (tooling, generated SDK clients)
- [/openapi.yaml](/openapi.yaml) — same spec, YAML-encoded (LLM context, PR diffs)
- SDK reference — `docs/sdk-reference.md` *(arrives in v0.9.7.4)*

## Plugin routes

Plugins can register their own HTTP routes. By default these are NOT shown
here (the spec only documents the **platform** API, not individual plugins).
Plugin authors can opt in by setting `openapi_public: true` in their
`plugin.yaml`.

## Versioning

The platform is pre-1.0. Breaking changes are documented in the
[CHANGELOG](https://github.com/nousviz/nousviz-app/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md).

post.summary: Execute Query
post.description: Execute a read-only SQL query against the specified engine.

get.summary: List installed plugins
get.description:
List only active (installed) plugins — used by the Installed Plugins page and sidebar.

B144 (v0.9.2.4): each entry carries an `update_status` block from the
plugin_update_status cache. Stale entries (older than ~1h) trigger a
fire-and-forget refresh in the background so the next call sees fresh
data. The current call doesn't block on the network check.

Keystone B (Phase 12 perf, v0.10.0.5.6): the catalog + last-sync
lookups that `_enrich_datasets` used to fire per-plugin are now
pre-fetched in two batched calls before the loop. Drops `/api/plugins`
DB round trips from ~6N to ~3 for the enrichment block alone.

B305 (v0.10.0.6): the result list is filtered through
`rbac.filter_plugins_for_user` so a viewer/analyst with a per-user
allowlist (resource_acls rows for resource_type='plugin') sees only
their permitted set + utilities. Admins/superadmins bypass.

get.summary: Recent plugin lifecycle events (install/uninstall/update/etc.)
get.description: View plugin audit log entries.

get.summary: Capabilities registered by installed utility plugins
get.description: Return registered capabilities from installed utility plugins.

get.summary: Full plugin catalog for the Marketplace page
get.description:
Full plugin catalog — official + installed + community, with installed flag.
Used by the Marketplace page.

Priority: installed/ and community/ win over official/ stubs for the same
plugin slug — the installed copy has richer metadata and is the live version.
Official stubs only appear in the catalog when the plugin is not installed.

P20b: merges install_count, featured, listed, pricing_model from plugin_registry.
Plugins with listed=false are excluded. Sorted: featured first, then by install_count desc.

get.summary: Get a plugin's full manifest with module merges + predicate resolution
get.description:
Get full plugin manifest, with enabled module manifests merged in.

v0.8.6: also resolves P119 action predicates and P121 checklist
predicates server-side so the frontend can render without further
round trips.

v0.9.0 (P204): includes `load_status` reflecting whether the
plugin's api/routes.py loaded successfully at API startup. If false,
`failure_reason` explains why (ModuleNotFoundError, SyntaxError, etc).

get.summary: Get a plugin dashboard spec (YAML, returned verbatim)
get.description: Get a dashboard spec for rendering. Searches parent dashboards/ first, then enabled modules.

404.description: Dashboard not found in plugin or its enabled modules.

get.summary: Get a plugin dataset schema (YAML, returned verbatim)
get.description: Get a dataset schema.

post.description: Test connectivity to a private repo before installing. Probe-clones and reads manifest.

403.description: Caller lacks the plugins.install permission, or SSH auth failed.

post.summary: Install a plugin
post.description:
Install a plugin. Three-tier source resolution (P19):

- Tier 1 (official): no repository_url — clones github.com/nousviz/plugin-{slug}
- Tier 2 (community): no repository_url — reads URL from plugins/community/{slug}/plugin.yaml
- Tier 3 (private): explicit repository_url in request body

Idempotent if already installed. Restart the API after installing to activate routes.

Security (P22):
- Rate limited: 5 installs per 5 minutes per IP (G3)
- Git clone pins to declared version tag (G2) — refuses HEAD installs
- pip runs with a sanitised environment — no NOUSVIZ_* vars exposed (G5)
- repository_url validated against SSRF blocklist (P19)

503.description: nousviz_sdk not importable in the API runtime.

delete.summary: Uninstall a plugin (with optional dependent cascade)
delete.description:
Uninstall a plugin.

- remove_data=true: run down migrations to drop plugin tables before removal
- remove_references=true (B281, v0.9.11.21): auto-clean orphaned
  references — delete annotations pinned to the plugin, delete
  shares pointing at /plugin/<id>/*, strip the plugin slug from
  fusion `requires` arrays. Alert rules are left alone (Phase 2).
- cascade=true: also uninstall all plugins that depend on this one

Returns has_dependents status if dependents exist and cascade=false —
the frontend should prompt the user to confirm cascade or cancel.

get.summary: Pre-uninstall info for the confirmation modal
get.description:
Return information needed to render the uninstall confirmation modal:
- dependents: installed plugins that depend on this one (via `requires.{capability}`)
- tables: Postgres/ClickHouse tables owned by this plugin
- data_dirs: filesystem data directories (mainly for utility plugins)

get.summary: Bulk update status for every installed plugin (B144 cache)
get.description:
Bulk fetch cached update status for every installed plugin.

Stale entries (older than ~1h) get a fire-and-forget refresh kicked
off in the background so the UI sees fresh data on the next poll.

post.summary: Force a synchronous update check for one plugin
post.description:
Synchronously check this plugin for an available update.

Use when the operator clicks an explicit "Check now" affordance, or
when the cached status says no-update but the operator wants to force
a re-check after pushing a new version upstream.

post.summary: Atomic-swap update to the latest version (B145)
post.description:
Update an installed plugin to the latest version from its source.

Atomic-swap design (B145): the new code is cloned to a staging directory
first, validated, then atomically swapped with the live install. If
anything fails before the swap, the live install is untouched. If the
post-swap idempotent steps (migrations, grants) fail, the previous live
install is restored from a sibling backup.

Credentials, settings, and synced data are preserved across the swap
(DB tables are not dropped). The plugin briefly becomes unavailable
while PM2 reloads to pick up the new routes.

get.description: Get an alert definition.

get.description: Return current saved settings for a plugin.

get.description: List modules for a plugin with their enabled state.

post.description: Enable a plugin module.

post.description: Disable a plugin module. Data is preserved.

get.description: Return connection config for a plugin including module connections. Masks secrets.

post.description: Save connection config for a plugin. Secrets → encrypted DB. Non-secrets → .env.

400.description: Plugin has no connection config declared.

get.summary: Sync status snapshot for the unified Sync card (B205)
get.description:
Sync status snapshot for the unified Sync card (B205, v0.9.6).

Returns:
    current: most recent run with status IN ('queued','running','cancelling'),
        including live progress JSONB and elapsed seconds. None when idle.
    last_success: most recent successful run.
    last_failure: most recent failed run (error/timeout/cancelled).
    last_sync: ISO timestamp of last_success.completed_at — kept for
        backward compatibility with pre-v0.9.6 frontend code.

The legacy plugin_settings._last_sync fallback is removed in v0.9.6 —
job_runs is the single source of truth.

get.summary: Composite read of manifest + override + scheduler state
get.description:
Composite read: manifest cron + override cron + scheduler registry state.

Used by the Settings tab's schedule override block (B148).

post.summary: Write or clear the per-plugin schedule override
post.description:
Write a per-plugin schedule override.

Body forms (mutually exclusive):
  {"cron": "0 */12 * * *"}                 raw cron
  {"interval_value": 15, "interval_unit": "minutes"}  friendly form
  {"cron": null} or {"cron": ""}           clear override

The scheduler observes the change on its next poll (within ~30s).
To make the change visible immediately, we delete the registry row;
the scheduler re-creates it on next poll with the new effective cron.

400.description: Invalid cron, invalid interval, or both forms supplied.

get.summary: Plugin's declared React components + trust state (B151)
get.description:
List a plugin's declared frontend components and their trust state.

Public-ish: any logged-in user can read (the frontend needs this at boot
to know which components to dynamically import). Doesn't expose secrets.

post.summary: Operator consent: trust this plugin's React components
post.description:
Operator consent: set plugin_settings._trust_frontend = true.

Required before the frontend will dynamically import this plugin's
declared React components. Audited via plugin_audit_log so revocation
history is queryable.

post.summary: Operator revoke: clear plugin frontend trust
post.description:
Operator revoke: clear plugin_settings._trust_frontend.

Idempotent — calling on an already-untrusted plugin returns success.

get.summary: Serve a plugin's bundled JS widget asset
get.description:
Serve a plugin's bundled JS widget file.

Refuses unless:
  - Plugin exists + has a manifest
  - Plugin is trusted (operator consented via /trust-frontend)
  - Filename matches a declared component's path basename
  - File exists on disk inside the plugin dir
  - Resolved path is contained within the plugin dir (no traversal)

403.description: Plugin frontend not trusted by operator.

404.description: Plugin not installed or filename not declared.

get.summary: Overall instance health
get.description:
Overall health check for the NousViz instance.

Public endpoint (no auth required) — this is the same shape used by
load balancers and the operator dashboard. The response is intentionally
nested: `services.postgres` reports DB connectivity and critical-table
presence, `runtime.sdk` reports whether `nousviz_sdk` imported, and
`stats` carries operator-dashboard counts.

Top-level `status` flips to `degraded` when Postgres reports degraded,
the SDK is unavailable, or critical tables are missing. Frontend
`evaluateChecks` drives banner display from this shape — additive
changes here are safe; renaming or removing fields will break the UI.

get.summary: Boolean status of security-sensitive config
get.description:
Return boolean status of security-sensitive config values.

Public endpoint (no auth required) — this is what the dashboard
config-banner reads to decide whether to nudge the operator about
missing encryption keys, missing superadmin user, SMTP config, etc.

**Never exposes actual values** — only whether they are set and
non-default. The `update_*` fields surface a once-per-hour-cached
GitHub release check so operators can see the "update available"
banner without polling the GitHub API on every request.

get.summary: Plugin connection health issues
get.description:
Banner-shaped list of plugin connection-health issues.

Each entry carries a plugin id, severity, message, and optional
structured detail — the UI renders each as a banner on the home
dashboard. An empty list means all installed plugins report healthy
connections.

Plugins will register their own health checks via `plugin_registry`
in a future release; the current implementation always returns no
issues.

get.summary: Recent health-check snapshots from health_log
get.description: Return health check history from the health_log table.

post.summary: Run a health check + persist to health_log (PM2 cron + manual refresh)
post.description:
Run a health check and store the result in health_log.

Accepted from:
- localhost (PM2 cron on the same box) — unlimited rate
- authenticated requests (session token, API key, or Cloudflare) — lets
  an operator force a fresh check from the browser via the Refresh button
  on /health-overview. Rate-limited per-IP.

403.description: Health record requires localhost or authenticated access.

post.summary: Provision Let's Encrypt SSL via the ssl-setup.sh script
post.description:
Run SSL setup from the UI. Calls ssl-setup.sh as subprocess.
Only Let's Encrypt is supported (requires a domain).

post.summary: Trigger Sync
post.description:
Trigger a plugin sync manually.

B205 (v0.9.6): always async. Manifest `execution_mode` is honored for
scheduled runs (the scheduler dispatches them) but ignored here —
manual triggers always enqueue and return immediately so the HTTP
request never blocks on subprocess execution. The unified Sync card
on the plugin Settings tab polls /sync/status for live progress.

Returns 409 Conflict when an active run already exists (status in
queued/running/cancelling). Body shape on 409:
    {"detail": {"run_id": <existing>, "status": <status>,
                "already_running": true}}
Frontend swaps to the live progress view in this case rather than
enqueueing a duplicate.

post.summary: Run a plugin's setup_schema.py script (60s timeout)
post.description:
Run the plugin's schema setup script.

Synchronous (not part of the async sync pipeline) — the response
blocks until the subprocess exits or the 60s timeout fires. The
plugin's environment is sanitised by `plugin_subprocess.plugin_sync_env`.

post.summary: Run a plugin's health_check.py script (30s timeout)
post.description:
Run the plugin's health check script.

Synchronous — the response blocks until the subprocess exits or
the 30s timeout fires. The plugin's environment is sanitised by
`plugin_subprocess.plugin_sync_env`.

Restore the annotation to its state before the last change.

Two outcomes: undoing a 'created' action archives the annotation
(since there's nothing to restore to); undoing an 'updated' action
restores the previous snapshot and removes that history entry.

post.summary: Quick semantic score (useful | neutral | useless)
post.description: Quick-score an annotation as useful / neutral / useless.

get.description: List all alerts, with human-readable frequency and period labels.

post.summary: Create an alert rule
post.description: Create a new alert.

get.summary: Available data sources for alert configuration (grouped by origin)
get.description:
Return available data sources for alert configuration, grouped by origin:
- postgres: Platform tables in the public schema (with columns + row counts)
- plugin: Datasets declared in installed plugin manifests
- connection: External data sources registered in the connections table

put.summary: Patch an alert (partial — null fields skipped)
put.description: Update an alert (toggle, change threshold, etc.).

post.summary: Dry-run an alert against current data
post.description:
Test-run an alert against current data without triggering notifications.

Imports the worker's evaluator at request time. If the worker module
isn't importable on this server, returns `{error: ...}` instead of
a fired/checked breakdown.

get.summary: Per-day trigger counts + semantic-score sparkline
get.description: Return per-day trigger counts + dominant semantic score for the last N days.

get.summary: List notes (pinned-first, optional page-path/plugin filter)

post.summary: Create a note attached to a page (and optional date range)

put.summary: Update a note (partial — null fields skipped)

post.summary: Record a user activity event
post.description:
Record a user activity event with device and IP metadata.

Open to any authenticated user (POST-only — they can log their own
activity but can't read the firehose).

get.summary: List recent activity events (admin-only firehose)
get.description:
List recent activity. Newest-first, optional filters on action /
plugin_id / page_path.

get.summary: Per-page and per-plugin usage analytics
get.description:
Analytics: which dashboards are being used?

Aggregates page_view events into per-page + per-plugin counts plus
a daily-activity histogram. `unused_dashboards` enumerates manifest-
declared dashboard paths that received zero views in the period.

get.summary: Admin analytics: time, devices, IPs, sessions
get.description:
Admin analytics: time spent, devices, IPs, sessions, and usage patterns.

Time-spent is a heuristic — sums gaps between consecutive page_view
events, capped at 30 minutes per gap so an idle tab doesn't inflate
the total. Sessions are runs of page_views separated by gaps >= 30 min.

get.summary: List all shared links (active and revoked)

post.summary: Create a shareable link (optional password + expiry)

get.summary: Public metadata for a share landing page

410.description: Link has been revoked or has expired.

patch.summary: Update a share link's title and/or notes

404.description: Share link not found or already revoked.
Share link not found or already revoked.

post.summary: Access a share link (public; password-gated when applicable)

get.summary: Last 50 access attempts for a share link
get.description: Return access log for a share link.

get.summary: List all scheduled jobs with status + cron source
get.description:
Return all known scheduled jobs with status + schedule source.

Aggregates plugin sync jobs (one per installed plugin), the alert
runner, and the system health monitor. `cron_source` distinguishes
PM2-scheduled vs crontab-scheduled deployments — the frontend uses
it to show the right "how to schedule" hint.

get.summary: List recent job runs
get.description:
List recent job runs, optionally filtered by job_id.

Returns up to `limit` runs ordered by `started_at` DESC. Used by
the System → Jobs page and the per-plugin Sync history block.

get.summary: Centralized job state — running / recent / upcoming / failing (B277)
get.description:
Return the unified job-state snapshot rendered on /system/jobs.

Sections:
  - now: currently-running + queued jobs with elapsed_ms and
    will_overlap_next (elapsed already exceeds the gap to next fire)
  - recent: last 12h of completed runs ordered by started_at DESC
  - upcoming: next 6h of scheduled fires with may_overlap predictions
  - failing: jobs with > 50% error rate (min 4 runs) over 24h

Cached in-process for 30 seconds; pass `?fresh=true` to bypass.

get.summary: Detail for a single job run
get.description: Return detail for a single job_runs row — status, progress, heartbeat.

post.summary: Cancel a queued or running job run
post.description:
Cancel a queued or running run. Cooperative — the plugin sees the
cancel via check_cancelled() on its next poll.

- queued  → status='cancelled' (never ran)
- running → status='cancelling' (plugin exits on next check_cancelled)
- paused  → status='cancelled'
- terminal (success/error/timeout/cancelled/skipped) → 200 no-op

`?force=true` (B277 v0.9.11.16.3+): force-marks the run terminal as
`cancelled` regardless of current status (skipping the cooperative
`cancelling` state). Used for **orphaned runs** where the worker is
confirmed gone — e.g. after a Postgres restart or scheduler crash
that left rows in `'running'` without any process actively executing
them. Without `?force=true`, those rows would hang in `cancelling`
forever (no worker to observe the cancel).

**Server-gated liveness check (v0.9.11.16.4)**: when `?force=true`
is passed against a `'running'` row whose `heartbeat_at` is fresh
(worker heartbeated within the last 90 seconds), the request is
refused with 409. The worker is alive — cooperative cancel will
work, and force-cancel would create a status mismatch where the
worker still thinks it's running. The dashboard frontend uses
`JobsDashboardNowItem.worker_alive` to pick the right button
automatically; this server-side check is the safety net.

409.description: Run is in a status that can't be cancelled.

post.summary: Pause a queued or running job run
post.description:
Pause a running run. Plugin exits at next check_cancelled(); the
run lands in 'paused' status (not 'cancelled') so resume re-queues it.

- running → status='paused' (via cancelling — plugin exits cleanly)
- queued  → status='paused' directly (never claimed)

409.description: Run is in a status that can't be paused.

post.summary: Re-queue a paused job run
post.description:
Re-queue a paused run so the worker picks it up fresh.

Only valid transition: paused → queued.

409.description: Only paused runs can be resumed.

post.summary: Immediately trigger a schedulable job
post.description:
Immediately trigger a schedulable job.

For plugin syncs (job_id looks like '<plugin_id>-sync'), delegates to
the manual-trigger endpoint which honors execution_mode (async vs
sync). For core jobs (alerts-runner, health-monitor), this is a
no-op for now — their schedulers are external (PM2 cron_restart).

job_id comes from the `jobs` list `id` field (e.g. 'starter-plugin-sync').

400.description: Job is not fire-now capable (core jobs run on PM2).

post.summary: Upload a CSV dataset (multipart form)

get.summary: List dataset metadata (no data blob)
Get a dataset including its data matrix (sortable, paginated)
Auth-mode and current-session status
get.description:
Return auth-mode flags and (if a session token is present and
valid) the authenticated user.

Public endpoint — no auth required. The frontend calls this on
page load to decide whether to show the login screen, the setup
wizard (no users exist), or the dashboard.

post.summary: Register a new account (first user = superadmin; later users via invite)
Issue a session token for valid credentials
post.description: Authenticate and return a session token. Rate-limited per source IP.

post.summary: Accept an invite link and create the account

409.description: Email already exists — try logging in.

post.summary: Initiate password reset (enumeration-resistant)
post.description:
Public endpoint — initiate a password reset.

Always returns 200 with the same body regardless of whether the
email exists, matches a real user, or the email send actually
succeeded. This prevents user-enumeration via response timing or
response shape.

429.description: Rate-limited — too many resets for this email.

post.summary: Consume a reset token + set new password
post.description:
Public endpoint — consume a password reset token.

Validates the token, hashes the new password, updates the user row,
marks the token used, kills all sessions for the user.

Returns:
  200 {ok: true} on success
  400 {detail: {error: 'token_invalid' | 'token_expired' | 'token_used'}}
  400 if password too short

400.description: Token invalid/used/expired (structured detail) or password too short.

post.summary: Expire the current session
post.description:
Expire the session associated with the X-Session-Token header.

Idempotent — returns `{ok: true}` whether or not a valid token was
presented. Public endpoint.

post.summary: Re-authenticate for sensitive operations (B236)
post.description:
Re-authenticate the current session for sensitive operations.

Returns 200 with `step_up_until` on correct password, sets
user_sessions.step_up_until to now() + 5 minutes for the active session.

Wrong password returns 401 with the same error shape as /login. Subject
to the same per-IP rate limit as /login (5 attempts / 60s).

Requires an active session — returns 401 if no valid token.

post.summary: End impersonation by clearing session flags (B254 — no re-login)
post.description:
End the current impersonation by clearing flags on the caller's session.

B254 (v0.9.10.0.5): refactored from the v0.9.10.0 model where exit
killed the impersonation session row. The session row is now the
actor's existing session (with transient acting_as_* flags); exit
just clears the flags. The session token, expires_at, and metadata
all remain unchanged — actor stays logged in as themselves with no
re-login needed.

Declared BEFORE the `/impersonate/{user_id}` route so FastAPI's
first-match-wins resolution picks this for `/impersonate/exit`
rather than treating "exit" as a user_id parameter.

Idempotent: returns 200 with `wasImpersonating: false` if the
current session isn't impersonating.

No step-up requirement — anyone holding the session may leave the
impersonation. Step-up is required to ENTER impersonation, not exit.

post.summary: Start impersonating a user (B254 — sets session flag, no token swap)
post.description:
Start impersonating another user — by setting flags on the
caller's existing session, NOT by issuing a new session token.

B254 (v0.9.10.0.5): refactored from the v0.9.10.0 model where this
INSERTed a new short-lived session row and returned a new token.
Now updates the caller's session with `acting_as_user_id` and
`acting_as_until`. The caller's token is unchanged. On exit (or
auto-expire), the flags clear and the caller is back as themselves
without re-login.

Requirements:
- Caller has users.manage (gated by Depends-style routing — see register_route above)
- Caller has stepped up within the last STEP_UP_TTL_MINUTES
- Caller's role rank > target's role rank (strict)
- Target user exists and is active
- Caller cannot already be impersonating (must exit first)

Returns:
- 200 with `{acting_as: {target serialized}, acting_as_until: <iso>}`.
  Note: NO `token` field in the response. Caller's existing token
  continues to work; the next /api/auth/me will show the new
  `acting_as` field.
- 401 if not stepped up.
- 403 with `{error: 'rank_violation'}` if rank check fails.
- 404 if target not found.
- 409 if already impersonating.

403.description: Rank violation (actor's role rank not strictly greater than target's).

404.description: Target user not found or not active.

409.description: Caller is already impersonating — must exit first.

get.summary: Introspect a session token (public)
get.description:
Introspect a session token via the `?token=` query parameter.

Public endpoint — does not require X-Session-Token. Returns
`{valid: false}` for any invalid, expired, or missing token.
Used by share-link landing pages and embed contexts to test
whether a token is still good without consuming it.

get.summary: Current actor (with optional acting_as target)
get.description:
Public `GET /api/auth/me` endpoint — Option B identity shape.

B236 (v0.9.10.0): always returns the ACTOR (the human authenticated
to the session) as the primary identity. When the session is
impersonating, the response also carries an `acting_as` field with
the target's serialized identity.

Frontend reads `me` for actor identity (audit, "Exit impersonation"
banner, log-out button) and `me.acting_as` for effective identity
(permission checks, role display). The `useEffectiveIdentity()` hook
centralizes the choice for permission/UI display.

patch.summary: Update own profile (password change requires step-up — B251)
patch.description:
Update the current user's profile.

B251 (v0.9.10.0.3): when the request includes the password field,
requires recent step-up auth (same gate as RBAC writes from B236).
Without this, a stolen session token could change the password and
lock the real owner out. Other fields (name, etc.) remain step-up-
free — they're not security-sensitive.

400.description: Password too short or empty body.

401.description: Step-up auth required for password change.

get.summary: Resolved permissions for the current effective user
get.description:
B230 (v0.9.8.3): return the set of permissions the current user
holds. The frontend uses this for role-aware UI (sidebar nav,
conditional buttons) without needing to duplicate the role-permission
catalog from rbac/permissions.py.

Resolves the user, looks up their role's permission set, and returns
a flat list. v0.9.9.x will layer DB overrides on top — this same
endpoint will return the resolved post-override set, so the frontend
contract doesn't change.

get.summary: Permissions held by an arbitrary role (admin preview UI)
get.description:
B231 (v0.9.8.4): return the permissions held by an arbitrary role.

Powers the admin-only "preview as <role>" UI — admins toggle the

get.summary: Paginated rows from a discovered plugin table (B262: server-side filters + search)
get.description:
Paginated rows from a discovered plugin table.

The catalog-driven replacement for /api/data-port/plugins/:slug/tab/:tabId.
Works for every plugin's every granted table — no `dataport.yaml`
opt-in required.

`sort` accepts "column" or "column desc" / "column asc". Invalid
sort (column not in table) is silently dropped (no-sort fallback)
rather than 400-erroring; pagination still works.

`q` is a server-side substring search. Casts text-coercible columns
to text and matches via ILIKE. Empty q is treated as no q.

`filter` is repeatable. Each value is `col:op:value` (or
`col:is_null` / `col:not_null` for null checks). Filters AND together;
the response's `total` reflects the filtered count.

Response:
    {
      "rows": [{...}, {...}, ...],
      "total": 7068,    # filtered count when q/filter present
      "page": 1,
      "limit": 50
    }

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Full-dataset substring search (B262). Matches via ILIKE %q% across text-coercible columns (text, varchar, json, jsonb, uuid). Capped at 100 characters.
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Full-dataset substring search (B262). Matches via ILIKE %q% across text-coercible columns (text, varchar, json, jsonb, uuid). Capped at 100 characters.

parameters.name: filter

schema.description: Per-column predicate filter (B262). Repeatable. Each filter is `col:op:value`. Operators: eq, neq, gt, lt, gte, lte, contains, startswith, is_null, not_null. Up to 8 per request. Filters compose with AND.
schema.title: Filter

parameters.description: Per-column predicate filter (B262). Repeatable. Each filter is `col:op:value`. Operators: eq, neq, gt, lt, gte, lte, contains, startswith, is_null, not_null. Up to 8 per request. Filters compose with AND.

400.description: Malformed filter, unknown column/operator, q too long, or too many filters.

get.description:

Backs the Users tab on the matrix page. For each user, returns:
  - identity (id, email, name, role, is_active)
  - their resolved permission set (from role -> permissions catalog)
  - their most-recent allow decision in auth_audit (last 30d)

v0.9.8.x has no DB overrides yet. v0.9.9.x will layer overrides on
top — this endpoint will return the post-override resolved set so
the frontend contract doesn't change.

If `permissions` is omitted and `based_on` is a built-in role, the
new role's permission set starts as a copy of that role's defaults.
If both are omitted, the role starts empty and overrides must be
added separately via /role-overrides.

B236 (v0.9.10.0): sensitive permissions in the explicit seed list are
rejected with 409 (was silently filtered before — operators were
creating roles thinking they got the requested permissions).
`based_on` seeds still filter sensitive perms from the source role's
defaults, since the operator didn't ask for them explicitly.

Full dashboard row.

`widgets` and `layout` are JSONB blobs whose shape is defined by the
dashboard editor / widget runtime. We accept them verbatim with
`extra='allow'` covering any future top-level columns.

DashboardSummary.title: DashboardSummary
DashboardSummary.description:
Compact dashboard row from the list endpoint — widgets blob
replaced by `widget_count`.

DashboardUsageResponse.title: DashboardUsageResponse
DashboardUsageResponse.description:
GET /api/activity/dashboard-usage — analytics aggregate.

`unused_dashboards` enumerates manifest-declared dashboard paths
that received zero page_view events in the period.

DatabaseSaveResponse.title: DatabaseSaveResponse
DatabaseSaveResponse.description:
POST /api/settings/database — confirms write + post-write probe.

`version` is the live Postgres version when the new connection
succeeds. `error` carries the failure message when the new config
can't connect (the .env was still patched — operator can fix and
retry).

DatabaseSettingsResponse.title: DatabaseSettingsResponse
DatabaseSettingsResponse.description: GET /api/settings/database — current Postgres config without password.

DataportPluginConfigResponse.title: DataportPluginConfigResponse
DataportPluginConfigResponse.description:
GET /api/data-port/plugins/{plugin_slug} — full dataport.yaml.

Schema is plugin-author-defined; we accept it verbatim.

DataportPluginIndexEntry.title: DataportPluginIndexEntry
DataportPluginIndexEntry.description: A single plugin in the dataport index — slug + tab labels.

DataportPluginsListResponse.title: DataportPluginsListResponse
DataportPluginsListResponse.description: GET /api/data-port/plugins — installed plugins that ship dataport.yaml.

DataportTabIndexEntry.title: DataportTabIndexEntry
DataportTabIndexEntry.description: Compact tab descriptor used in the dataport plugin index.

DataportTabRowsResponse.title: DataportTabRowsResponse
DataportTabRowsResponse.description:
GET /api/data-port/plugins/{plugin_slug}/tab/{tab_id}.

Paginated rows from the tab's declared SQL table. `rows` is a list
of plugin-table-shaped dicts (column types vary per table), so
typed as `list[dict[str, Any]]` rather than a fixed shape.

data.title: Data
data.description: Row-major matrix; inner list values match `columns` ordering.

DatasetDetailResponse.title: DatasetDetailResponse
DatasetDetailResponse.description:
GET /api/datasets/{slug} — full dataset including the `data` blob.

Sort + paginate happens server-side; `total_rows` is the unpaginated
count, `data` carries the (possibly sliced) row matrix.

columns.title: Columns
columns.description: Column names in the order they appeared in the source CSV.

column_types.title: Column Types
column_types.description: Inferred type per column ('number' | 'string' | etc.) — used for sort + render hints.

DatasetSummary.title: DatasetSummary
DatasetSummary.description: A single datasets row in the list response — metadata only, no `data` blob.

DatasetUploadResponse.title: DatasetUploadResponse
DatasetUploadResponse.description:
POST /api/datasets/upload — newly stored dataset row.

Same shape as DatasetSummary plus the `id` returned by the
upsert.

DatasetsListResponse.title: DatasetsListResponse
DatasetsListResponse.description: GET /api/datasets — metadata for every dataset, newest-first.

DeployKeyCheckResponse.title: DeployKeyCheckResponse
DeployKeyCheckResponse.description:
GET /api/settings/deploy-keys/check — does a key exist for `repo_url`?

`match='repo'` indicates an exact-URL match (B204). The legacy
host-fallback was removed; only exact URL hits return has_key=True.

DeployKeyCreateResponse.title: DeployKeyCreateResponse

DeployKeyCreator.title: DeployKeyCreator
DeployKeyCreator.description: Joined creator info on deploy_keys rows.

created_by.description: The actor who created this key. Null if the user has been deleted.

DeployKeyEntry.title: DeployKeyEntry
DeployKeyEntry.description: A single deploy_keys row as returned by GET /api/settings/deploy-keys.

detail.title: Detail
detail.description: Truncated SSH output (200 chars) or error description.

DeployKeyTestResponse.title: DeployKeyTestResponse
DeployKeyTestResponse.description:
POST /api/settings/deploy-keys/{key_id}/test — SSH-auth probe.

`ok=True` means GitHub responded 'successfully authenticated'. The
`detail` carries either the short SSH stderr or a timeout / failure
description.

channel_type.title: Channel Type
channel_type.description: Channel type from webhook_endpoints: generic / slack / discord / teams.

subscribed.title: Subscribed
subscribed.description: True iff the operator has explicitly subscribed this webhook to diagnostic alerts.

DiagnosticAlertSubscription.title: DiagnosticAlertSubscription
DiagnosticAlertSubscription.description:
One outbound webhook + its diagnostic-alert subscription state.

v0.9.11.24 (B283) renamed `webhook_slug` → `webhook_id` and added
`channel_type`. Existing slug-keyed subscriptions were backfilled
by migration 070; the API now exposes the UUID directly.

delivered.title: Delivered
delivered.description: Webhooks the synthetic payload reached successfully.

first_detected_at.title: First Detected At
first_detected_at.description: Earliest snapshot in the queried window where the finding was present. Null when the finding has never been present in the queried window.

slug.title: Slug
slug.description: URL-safe identifier, used as the path segment.

section.title: Section
section.description: Top-level grouping for the docs sidebar.

available.title: Available
available.description: True iff the markdown file exists on disk.

DocEntry.title: DocEntry
DocEntry.description: Index entry for a documentation page.

DocsListResponse.title: DocsListResponse
DocsListResponse.description: GET /api/docs — index of all documentation pages.

EmailTestResponse.title: EmailTestResponse
EmailTestResponse.description: POST /api/settings/email/test — send a test email + report outcome.

ErrorDetail.title: ErrorDetail
ErrorDetail.description:
Generic 4xx response. The `detail` field is a human-readable
string describing the error (FastAPI's default HTTPException shape).

id.title: Id
id.description: Stable rule identifier (used for dedup, history lookup).

evidence.title: Evidence
evidence.description: 2-4 lines explaining what was measured and why it triggered the rule.

recommendation.title: Recommendation
recommendation.description: Plain-language guidance — what to do about it.

last_alerted_at.title: Last Alerted At
last_alerted_at.description: B274 (v0.9.11.20): ISO timestamp of the most recent webhook alert dispatched for this (finding_id, affected_key). Null when no alert has fired (severity below threshold, or the subscription set is empty). Drives the `alert sent N min ago` badge on the FindingCard.

Finding.title: Finding
Finding.description: One actionable issue surfaced by the diagnostic engine.

FindingAction.title: FindingAction
FindingAction.description:
Action button on a finding card.

Phase 1 (v0.9.11.18) supports `external` (link) and `manual`
(copy-to-clipboard SQL/shell). The Phase 2 `sql_with_confirmation`
type — execute privileged DROP / VACUUM via a confirmation modal
— is deferred pending its own audit + RBAC review.

FindingAffected.title: FindingAffected
FindingAffected.description:
One thing the finding is about (a table, sync, index, etc.).

`detail` is freeform plain-language extra context (size, row count,
timestamp). The frontend renders these as small chips on the
expanded card — they're meant to give the operator enough context
to act without expanding further.

severity.title: Severity
severity.description: Severity at this snapshot. Null when present=false.

FindingHistoryPoint.title: FindingHistoryPoint
FindingHistoryPoint.description: One sample in a finding presence time-series.

FireNowResponse.title: FireNowResponse
FireNowResponse.description:
POST /api/jobs/{job_id}/fire-now response.

For plugin sync jobs, this returns the same shape as POST
/api/plugins/{id}/sync (the SyncResponse from B205). The fields
here mirror that shape with `extra='allow'` to absorb any keys the
underlying handler adds.

components.title: Components
components.description: React components declared in the plugin's frontend.components manifest block.

admin_proxy.title: Admin Proxy
admin_proxy.description: B304 (v0.10.0.5): plugin opts into the path-scoped admin-session cookie auth path. When true, the auth middleware accepts a nv_admin_<slug> cookie for requests under /api/plugins/<slug>/admin/* in addition to the existing X-Session-Token / X-API-Key headers. Cookies are minted by the plugin's own bridge endpoint via nousviz_sdk.auth.issue_admin_session_cookie(). Default false: middleware enforces header-based auth as today.

FrontendComponent.title: FrontendComponent
FrontendComponent.description: Single frontend component declared in plugin.yaml (B151).

FrontendComponentEntry.title: FrontendComponentEntry
FrontendComponentEntry.description: Augmented component entry — manifest declaration + on-disk presence.

GenericMessageResponse.title: GenericMessageResponse
GenericMessageResponse.description:
Generic `{ok, message}` response — used by forgot-password and
reset-password to keep the response shape constant across success
and silent-no-op paths (enumeration resistance).

github_token_set.title: Github Token Set
github_token_set.description: True iff GITHUB_TOKEN is set in the environment.

github_token_preview.title: Github Token Preview
github_token_preview.description: Masked preview of the token. Empty string when no token is set.

GitSettingsGetResponse.title: GitSettingsGetResponse
GitSettingsGetResponse.description:
GET /api/settings/git — boolean status + masked token preview.

Never exposes the full token. The `github_token_preview` field is
`<first8>...<last4>` for tokens longer than 12 chars, or '••••'
when only short/redacted tokens are stored.

superadmin_exists.title: Superadmin Exists
superadmin_exists.description: True iff at least one superadmin user row exists.

postgres_password_is_default.title: Postgres Password Is Default
postgres_password_is_default.description: Always False since S108 (v0.8.1) — kept for response-shape back-compat.

update_available.title: Update Available
update_available.description: True iff a newer release is available on GitHub.

HealthConfigResponse.title: HealthConfigResponse
HealthConfigResponse.description: Boolean status of security-sensitive config — never the values themselves.

checks.title: Checks
checks.description: JSONB array of check-result dicts (id, status, label, detail).

HealthLogRow.title: HealthLogRow
HealthLogRow.description: A single health_log entry — a snapshot of a periodic check.

checks.title: Checks
checks.description: Count of checks in this snapshot.

startup_time.title: Startup Time
startup_time.description: When this API process started, ISO-8601.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: When this response was generated, ISO-8601.

services.title: Services
services.description: Per-service health blocks. The 'postgres' key always exists with shape {status, version?, tables?, critical_tables_present?, critical_tables_total?, missing_critical_tables?, drift_hint?}. Utility-plugin entries have shape {status, version?}.

runtime.title: Runtime
runtime.description: Runtime check blocks. Currently contains 'sdk' with shape {status, version, import_error?}.

ssl.description: SSL config status. Present iff NOUSVIZ_SSL is set in the environment.

HealthResponse.title: HealthResponse
HealthResponse.description:
Top-level /health payload.

Status is degraded when Postgres reports degraded, the SDK is
unavailable, or critical tables are missing. Frontend `evaluateChecks`
drives banner display from this shape.

value.title: Value
value.description: Metric value at this snapshot. Null when the metric isn't applicable to the snapshot — e.g. a plugin that wasn't yet installed. The UI renders nulls as gaps, not zero.

HistoryPoint.title: HistoryPoint
HistoryPoint.description: One sample in a metric time-series.

ImpersonateStartResponse.title: ImpersonateStartResponse
ImpersonateStartResponse.description:
POST /api/auth/impersonate/{user_id}.

NOTE: no `token` field. The caller's existing session token is
reused with `acting_as_user_id` set on the session row (B254).

is_primary.title: Is Primary
is_primary.description: True for primary-key indexes. Surfaced so the unused_index diagnostic rule can exclude PKs (load-bearing for INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE + foreign-key lookups regardless of idx_scan count).

is_unique.title: Is Unique
is_unique.description: True for unique indexes (including PKs). Same exclusion rationale as is_primary — unique indexes enforce a constraint, not just speed up lookups.

InsightEntry.title: InsightEntry
InsightEntry.description:
Single insight from a Tier 1 (YAML) or Tier 2 (plugin endpoint) source.

Insight shape is plugin-author-defined; the consistent envelope is
`severity` + a free-form payload. extra='allow' covers
plugin-specific fields like `metric`, `evidence`, `actions`, etc.

InsightsListResponse.title: InsightsListResponse
InsightsListResponse.description:
GET /api/insights — aggregated insights from all installed plugins.

Sorted by severity (critical → warning → info → good → tip), then
truncated to `limit`. `total` is the un-truncated count.

InviteCreateResponse.title: InviteCreateResponse
InviteCreateResponse.description:
POST /api/auth/users/invite — invite issued.

`invite_url` is exposed only when email send failed (operator can
copy/paste the link). On successful send, it stays null and only
`email_sent=true` is reported.

InvitesListResponse.title: InvitesListResponse
InvitesListResponse.description: GET /api/auth/users/invites — recent invites with inviter info.

plugin_id.title: Plugin Id
plugin_id.description: '*' for any plugin, or a specific plugin slug.

on_status.title: On Status
on_status.description: Terminal statuses this subscription fires on (subset of error/timeout/cancelled).

webhook_channel_type.title: Webhook Channel Type
webhook_channel_type.description: Channel type of the referenced webhook (generic/slack/discord/teams). Null when the webhooks plugin is uninstalled (orphan subscription).

JobAlertSubscription.title: JobAlertSubscription
JobAlertSubscription.description:
One row in the job_alert_subscriptions table joined with the
referenced webhook's display info. webhook_name / webhook_url are
null when the webhooks plugin is uninstalled (orphan subscription).

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: 'Core' for built-in jobs, plugin display name otherwise.

recommended_schedule.title: Recommended Schedule
recommended_schedule.description: Cron expression suggested for this job.

cron_active.title: Cron Active
cron_active.description: True iff a cron entry was found scheduling this job.

manifest_schedule.title: Manifest Schedule
manifest_schedule.description: Cron from plugin.yaml. Plugin sync jobs only.

override.title: Override
override.description: True iff a per-plugin schedule override is set (B148). Plugin sync jobs only.

scheduler.description: v0.9.3 scheduler state for plugin sync jobs. Null for core jobs.

JobEntry.title: JobEntry
JobEntry.description:
Single row in /api/jobs response — one schedulable job.

Plugin sync jobs carry the additional fields `manifest_schedule`,
`override`, and `scheduler` (B150 — surfacing the v0.9.3 scheduler
state to the operator UI). Core jobs (alerts-runner, health-monitor)
omit those.

status.title: Status
status.description: The run's status after the operation.

JobRunControlResponse.title: JobRunControlResponse
JobRunControlResponse.description:
POST /api/jobs/{run_id}/{cancel|pause|resume} response.

`changed` is True when the operation moved the run into a new
status; False when the operation was a no-op (e.g. cancelling an
already-terminal run).

JobRunRow.title: JobRunRow
JobRunRow.description:
A single job_runs row — used by /api/jobs/runs and /api/jobs/{run_id}.

Datetimes are ISO-8601 strings. Extra fields are allowed because the
detail endpoint returns more columns than the list endpoint
(claimed_by, heartbeat_at, progress, etc).

age_sec.title: Age Sec
age_sec.description: Seconds since the registry row was last touched. <300 means scheduler is alive.

JobSchedulerState.title: JobSchedulerState
JobSchedulerState.description:
sync_schedule_registry row attached to plugin sync jobs (B150).

Surfaced under JobEntry.scheduler — tells the operator UI whether
the v0.9.3 scheduler is actively tracking this plugin and when it
last enqueued a run.

last_error_at.title: Last Error At
last_error_at.description: ISO timestamp of the most recent error — anchors the deep-link into /system/logs.

JobsDashboardFailingItem.title: JobsDashboardFailingItem
JobsDashboardFailingItem.description:
B277 (v0.9.11.16.1): a job with ANY errors in the last 24h.

Threshold widened from > 50% error rate to errors > 0 per operator
UX feedback: sporadic failures matter and should surface for
investigation. Ordered server-side by `last_error_at` DESC so the
frontend can lead with the most recent failure.

will_overlap_next.title: Will Overlap Next
will_overlap_next.description: True when elapsed already exceeds (next_fire_at - started_at).

heartbeat_at.title: Heartbeat At
heartbeat_at.description: ISO timestamp of the worker's most recent heartbeat write. Null until the row is claimed.

heartbeat_age_sec.title: Heartbeat Age Sec
heartbeat_age_sec.description: Seconds since heartbeat_at (server-computed). Null when heartbeat_at is null.

worker_alive.title: Worker Alive
worker_alive.description: True iff the worker heartbeated within the last 90s. Force-cancel is gated on this being false for running rows.

JobsDashboardNowItem.title: JobsDashboardNowItem
JobsDashboardNowItem.description:
B277: a row in the dashboard's NOW section — a currently-running
or queued job with elapsed time + collision-prediction context.

v0.9.11.16.4 adds heartbeat liveness so callers can distinguish
a live worker from an orphaned 'running' row.

error_short.title: Error Short
error_short.description: First 200 chars of the run's error column, or null.

JobsDashboardRecentItem.title: JobsDashboardRecentItem
JobsDashboardRecentItem.description: B277: a completed job_runs row from the recent-history window.

JobsDashboardResponse.title: JobsDashboardResponse
JobsDashboardResponse.description:
B277: GET /api/jobs/dashboard — 4-section centralized job state.

Each section is independently sized, so callers can render whichever
blocks have content. `collected_at` lets the client tell when a
cached vs fresh snapshot is being shown.

may_overlap.title: May Overlap
may_overlap.description: True when avg_duration_ms exceeds 90% of ms_until_fire.

JobsDashboardUpcomingItem.title: JobsDashboardUpcomingItem
JobsDashboardUpcomingItem.description: B277: an upcoming scheduled fire with collision prediction.

crontab.title: Crontab
crontab.description: System crontab entries containing 'nousviz' — empty on PM2 deployments.

pm2.title: Pm2
pm2.description: PM2-managed processes with cron_restart — empty on crontab-only deployments.

JobsListResponse.title: JobsListResponse
JobsListResponse.description:
GET /api/jobs — every known scheduled job + crontab/PM2 metadata.

`cron_source` flips between 'crontab' and 'pm2' to drive the
frontend's "how to schedule" hint.

recent_data_changes.title: Recent Data Changes
recent_data_changes.description: Per-plugin sync recency (job_runs success + plugin_settings._last_sync union).

alerts_summary.description: Alert-system counts (total / enabled / triggered_24h) + recent triggers.

needs_attention.title: Needs Attention
needs_attention.description: System-level items needing operator action (e.g. expiring SSL, missing migrations).

LaunchpadResponse.title: LaunchpadResponse
LaunchpadResponse.description:
GET /api/launchpad — single-call data feed for the Overview page.

Each block is best-effort populated from a separate query inside the
handler; failures roll back the inner transaction and leave the
block at its empty default.

stage.title: Stage
stage.description: Where the loader was when it failed.

LoadStatus.title: LoadStatus
LoadStatus.description:
Plugin loader runtime state (P204).

`routes_registered=true` means the plugin's api/routes.py imported
cleanly at API startup. False means the loader caught an exception;
`failure_reason` carries the class + message (the full traceback
stays in app_logs for admin-visible debugging).

detail.title: Detail
detail.description: Structured JSONB detail payload — shape depends on the source.

LogEntry.title: LogEntry
LogEntry.description:
A single app_logs row as returned by /api/admin/logs.

`actor_email` and `run_status` are joined in from users / job_runs.
`actor_user_id` is the actor's UUID as a string (or null when the
log entry has no associated actor — e.g. system-emitted events).

LogFilterUser.title: LogFilterUser
LogFilterUser.description: Distinct actor for the /system/logs filter dropdown.

LogFiltersResponse.title: LogFiltersResponse
LogFiltersResponse.description:
GET /api/admin/logs/filters — distinct values for dropdown filters.

Limited to events from the last 30 days so the dropdowns don't
accumulate stale plugin slugs or deleted users.

token.title: Token
token.description: Raw session token. Send as X-Session-Token on subsequent requests.

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor
next_cursor.description: ID of the last entry; pass back as ?cursor=… to paginate.

LogsListResponse.title: LogsListResponse
LogsListResponse.description:
GET /api/admin/logs — paginated log feed.

Pagination is keyset on `id` descending. When `next_cursor` is
non-null, pass it back as the `cursor` query param to fetch the
next page. A null cursor means the response was shorter than
`limit` and there are no more rows.

acting_as.description: Target user when the session is impersonating; absent otherwise.

MeResponse.title: MeResponse
MeResponse.description:
GET /api/auth/me — Option B identity shape (B236).

The top-level fields describe the ACTOR (the human authenticated to
this session). `acting_as`, when present, carries the target the
actor is currently impersonating.

Frontend reads `me` for actor identity (for the impersonation
banner, audit display, log-out button) and `me.acting_as` for
effective identity (permission resolution, role display). The
`useEffectiveIdentity()` hook centralizes this choice.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: Sorted list of permission strings, e.g. ['plugins.install', 'system.logs'].

MyPermissionsResponse.title: MyPermissionsResponse
MyPermissionsResponse.description:
GET /api/auth/me/permissions — flat list of permissions for the
EFFECTIVE user (target if impersonating, actor otherwise).

The permission set is the resolved post-override set as of v0.9.9.x.

NoteEntry.title: NoteEntry
NoteEntry.description:
A single notes row.

POST and PUT return the raw row; LIST wraps in a `notes` envelope.
extra='allow' covers any future columns (e.g. tags) without a model bump.

NotesListResponse.title: NotesListResponse
NotesListResponse.description: GET /api/notes — pinned-first ordering, optional page-path/plugin filter.

PageViewEntry.title: PageViewEntry
PageViewEntry.description: Resolved page view used by the dashboard-usage analytics.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: Map of permission name -> {description, sensitive: bool}.

roles.title: Roles
roles.description: Backward-compatible flat map of role -> resolved permissions.

role_data.title: Role Data
role_data.description: Per-role metadata: kind (built_in|custom), display_name, default_permissions, resolved permissions, override deltas, and (for custom roles) created_by + created_at.

routes.title: Routes
routes.description: Each registered route's permission + per-role last-accessed timestamps.

public_routes.title: Public Routes
public_routes.description: Routes that bypass auth — list of [method, path] pairs.

audit_summary.title: Audit Summary
audit_summary.description: Allow/deny/shadow-mismatch counts + top-denial permissions over a window.

shadow_mode.title: Shadow Mode
shadow_mode.description: True iff RBAC is running in shadow mode (decisions logged but not enforced).

version.title: Version
version.description: Platform version string at the time of the snapshot.

PermissionsMatrixResponse.title: PermissionsMatrixResponse
PermissionsMatrixResponse.description:
GET /api/system/permissions — full RBAC registry snapshot.

Used by the audit matrix UI on /system/permissions. The deep
blocks (role_data, routes, audit_summary) are typed as
dict[str, Any] / list[dict[str, Any]] because they carry per-role
and per-route metadata whose shape varies (built-in vs custom
roles, overrides present vs absent, etc.).

role.title: Role
role.description: The target user's current role, for UI display.

unrestricted_by_role.title: Unrestricted By Role
unrestricted_by_role.description: True when the target user's role (admin/superadmin) makes them unrestricted regardless of ACL rows. UI greys out the editor.

PluginAccessResponse.title: PluginAccessResponse
PluginAccessResponse.description:
GET / PUT /api/auth/users/{user_id}/plugin-access — current
allowlist state for the user.

`mode='all'` means zero ACL rows (unrestricted). `mode='specific'`
means one or more rows; `plugin_ids` lists the slugs the user is
allowed to see (utility plugins always pass through regardless).

plugin.title: Plugin
plugin.description: Display name when known, slug otherwise.

PluginActivityEntry.title: PluginActivityEntry
PluginActivityEntry.description: Aggregate event count for one plugin.

PluginCapabilitiesResponse.title: PluginCapabilitiesResponse
PluginCapabilitiesResponse.description: GET /api/plugins/capabilities — capabilities registered by utility plugins.

PluginCatalogResponse.title: PluginCatalogResponse
PluginCatalogResponse.description:
GET /api/plugins/catalog — full plugin catalog (Marketplace page).

Combines official + installed + community + utilities. Each entry
includes installed flag, install_count, featured flag, pricing_model.
Sorted: featured first, then by install_count desc.

connections.title: Connections
connections.description: One entry per connection block in the plugin's manifest (typically one).

health.description: Result of the plugin's health_check hook, or null if none declared.

PluginConnectionsSaveResponse.title: PluginConnectionsSaveResponse
PluginConnectionsSaveResponse.description: POST /api/plugins/{id}/connections — confirms write + post-save health check.

PluginEntry.title: PluginEntry
PluginEntry.description:
Single plugin entry from /plugins or /plugins/{id}.

Carries the consistent envelope (id, version, display_name, status)
plus any number of plugin-author-defined fields (dashboards,
datasets, actions, settings, capabilities, …). The `extra='allow'`
config is intentional — plugin manifests are open-ended.

admin_proxy.title: Admin Proxy
admin_proxy.description: B304 (v0.10.0.5): plugin opts into the path-scoped admin-session cookie auth path. Surfaced here so the trust banner can render the admin-proxy consent line.

PluginInstallResponse.title: PluginInstallResponse
PluginInstallResponse.description:
POST /api/plugins/{id}/install — success path.

Returns `status='already_installed'` when the plugin's directory
already exists (idempotent). Otherwise `status='installed'` with
the manifest plus migrations + route-load status.

tables.title: Tables
tables.description: Postgres tables owned by this module (from module.yaml databases.postgres.tables).

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Setting declarations from module.yaml — shape varies.

PluginModuleEntry.title: PluginModuleEntry
PluginModuleEntry.description: A single plugin module (sub-package within a plugin).

output.title: Output
output.description: Combined stdout + stderr from the plugin script.

PluginScriptRunResponse.title: PluginScriptRunResponse
PluginScriptRunResponse.description:
Result of running a plugin's setup_schema.py or health_check.py.

Both endpoints return the same shape: subprocess exit code plus
combined stdout+stderr. `status` is 'success' on returncode 0,
'error' otherwise. Used by the plugin Settings tab to surface the
setup/health output to the operator.

value.title: Value
value.description: JSONB value — type depends on the plugin's setting declaration.

PluginSettingEntry.title: PluginSettingEntry
PluginSettingEntry.description: Single setting key/value pair from plugin_settings.

settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Saved settings, one entry per declared key in the manifest's settings block.

PluginSettingsResponse.title: PluginSettingsResponse
PluginSettingsResponse.description:
GET /api/plugins/{id}/settings — current saved settings.

`_conn.*` keys are excluded (they belong to the /connections surface).

data_removed.title: Data Removed
data_removed.description: Operator's checkbox state at uninstall time. For the actual outcome, see data_tables_dropped + data_tables_drop_failed.

data_tables_dropped.title: Data Tables Dropped
data_tables_dropped.description: Tables actually dropped (via manifest's databases.postgres.tables[]). Empty when remove_data=False or plugin declared no tables. Idempotent — re-uninstall produces the same list.

data_tables_drop_failed.title: Data Tables Drop Failed
data_tables_drop_failed.description: Per-table DROP failures with reason strings. Empty on success. Operators use this to manually clean up tables the platform couldn't drop.

migrations_run.title: Migrations Run
migrations_run.description: *_down.sql migration files executed (plugin author's intended cleanup). Defense-in-depth manifest-table drop in data_tables_dropped runs in addition.

references_removed.title: References Removed
references_removed.description: Operator's checkbox state at uninstall time. For the actual cleanup outcome, see references_cleanup.

references_cleanup.title: References Cleanup
references_cleanup.description: Per-kind cleanup outcomes when remove_references=true. Shape: {annotations_deleted: [{id, title}], shares_deleted: [{id, label}], fusions_repointed: [{id, name}], alerts_left_alone: [{id, name}], failed: [{kind, id, error}]}. Null when operator didn't opt in.

PluginUninstallResponse.title: PluginUninstallResponse
PluginUninstallResponse.description:
DELETE /api/plugins/{id}/install.

Two response shapes:
- `status='has_dependents'` (when other plugins depend on this one
  and `cascade=false`): the frontend should prompt the operator to
  confirm cascade or cancel. `dependents` lists the affected plugins.
- `status='uninstalled'` (success): lists what was removed and
  whether data was kept or dropped.

PluginUpdateInfo.title: PluginUpdateInfo
PluginUpdateInfo.description: Cached plugin update status for one plugin (B144).

PluginUpdatesListResponse.title: PluginUpdatesListResponse
PluginUpdatesListResponse.description: GET /api/plugins/updates — bulk status across every installed plugin.

PluginYamlResource.title: PluginYamlResource
PluginYamlResource.description:
A YAML-defined resource (alert, dashboard, dataset) returned verbatim.

Schema is plugin-author-defined; we return whatever the YAML deserialises to.

detail.title: Detail
detail.description: Permission-deny message naming the required permission.
examples: Permission denied: this action requires plugins.install.

RBACErrorDetail.title: RBACErrorDetail
RBACErrorDetail.description:
403 response from the RBAC layer (B227+). Same `detail` field but
with a documented format: `Permission denied: this action requires <permission>.`

target_resource_type.title: Target Resource Type
target_resource_type.description: B248 (v0.9.10.7): present on acl_grant / acl_revoke / set_default_policy rows.

target_resource_id.title: Target Resource Id
target_resource_id.description: B248 (v0.9.10.7): present on acl_grant / acl_revoke rows.

before_state.title: Before State
before_state.description: JSONB before-state — shape depends on the action.

after_state.title: After State
after_state.description: JSONB after-state — shape depends on the action.

RbacAuditEntry.title: RbacAuditEntry
RbacAuditEntry.description: Single rbac_config_audit row — one RBAC config mutation.

RbacAuditLogResponse.title: RbacAuditLogResponse
RbacAuditLogResponse.description:
GET /api/system/rbac-audit-log — paginated RBAC config mutations.

Pagination is keyset on `id` descending. Pass `next_cursor` back as
`?cursor=…` to fetch the next page.

ResourcesResponse.title: ResourcesResponse
ResourcesResponse.description: GET /api/system/resources — all server + Postgres + per-plugin metrics in one snapshot.

RestrictedUsersListResponse.title: RestrictedUsersListResponse
RestrictedUsersListResponse.description:
GET /api/auth/users/with-restricted-plugin-access — users whose
allowlist is non-empty AND does not include `exclude_slug`.

collected_at.title: Collected At
collected_at.description: ISO timestamp when this snapshot was assembled.

field.title: Field
field.description: Timestamp field used for the retention cutoff.

description.title: Description
description.description: Human-readable summary of what the policy keeps.

retention_days.title: Retention Days
retention_days.description: Current retention threshold in days. 0 means immediate purge of rows matching `additional_where`.

paused.title: Paused
paused.description: When true, the cron worker skips this policy. Default for every policy at install.

rows_total.title: Rows Total
rows_total.description: Current total rows in the policy's scope (bounded by additional_where if any).

rows_would_prune.title: Rows Would Prune
rows_would_prune.description: Rows that exceed the retention threshold and would be deleted on the next run.

RetentionPolicyState.title: RetentionPolicyState
RetentionPolicyState.description:
One row in the /settings/maintenance retention table.

`rows_total` and `rows_would_prune` are computed live (cached at
request time, no caching layer above) so the operator sees an
accurate "click 'Run now' and N rows will be deleted" preview.

summary.title: Summary
summary.description: Per-policy outcome. int = rows_deleted; 'paused' = skipped; 'error: <type>' = failed.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: Always True when this block is present.

expires.title: Expires
expires.description: Cert expiry as reported by `openssl x509 -enddate`. Present only when the cert is readable.

SSLBlock.title: SSLBlock
SSLBlock.description:
SSL config status when NOUSVIZ_SSL is set. Absent on HTTP-only deployments.

Shape mirrors `_get_ssl_status()` in routes/health.py — `enabled`
and `type` are always present; `domain` and `expires` are present
when applicable.

SaveConnectionsHealthBlock.title: SaveConnectionsHealthBlock
SaveConnectionsHealthBlock.description:
Health-check result attached to save-connections responses when
the plugin declares a `health_check:` hook.

ServerResources.title: ServerResources
ServerResources.description:
Server-level metrics. Fields are Optional because the API runs
on Linux production but also on macOS dev (no /proc/meminfo etc.).

SetupResponse.title: SetupResponse
SetupResponse.description:
POST /api/auth/setup — legacy first-admin creation.

extra='allow' covers whatever the legacy handler returns; this
surface is kept for backward compat and not actively maintained.

filters.title: Filters
filters.description: Free-form filter state (date range, dimension selections, etc.) — dashboard-author-defined shape.

ShareAccessResponse.title: ShareAccessResponse
ShareAccessResponse.description:
POST /api/shares/{share_id}/access — public landing-page access.

Returns the page-path + filters needed to render the shared view.
Filters is a free-form JSONB blob defined by the dashboard author.

url.title: Url
url.description: Relative URL of the share landing page (/shared/<id>).

ShareDetailResponse.title: ShareDetailResponse
ShareDetailResponse.description:
GET /api/shares/{share_id} — public metadata for the share landing page.

Returns 410 (gone) for revoked or expired links — the response below
is the success path only.

expired.title: Expired
expired.description: True iff the link's expiry has passed at query time.

SslSetupResponse.title: SslSetupResponse
SslSetupResponse.description:
POST /api/admin/ssl/setup — Let's Encrypt provisioning result.

On success, `ssl` carries the new SSL config (mirrors `_get_ssl_status`).
On failure, `reason` carries a machine-readable classification (e.g.
'timeout', 'dns_no_match') and `error` carries the human-readable message.

plugin_tables.title: Plugin Tables
plugin_tables.description: Count of plugin-managed tables in Postgres.

StatsBlock.title: StatsBlock
StatsBlock.description: Aggregate counts surfaced in /health for the operator dashboard.

StepUpRequiredDetail.title: StepUpRequiredDetail
StepUpRequiredDetail.description:
401 response from any endpoint gated by `requires_step_up`.
The `detail` field is a structured dict, not a string.

StepUpRequiredErrorBody.title: StepUpRequiredErrorBody
StepUpRequiredErrorBody.description:
The structured detail body returned by `requires_step_up` (B236)
and `PATCH /api/auth/me` with password (B251). Frontend's StepUpController
keys off `detail.error == 'stepup_required'` to pop the modal.

step_up_until.title: Step Up Until
step_up_until.description: ISO-8601 timestamp until which sensitive ops are unlocked (default 5 min).

progress.title: Progress
progress.description: Live progress payload from the worker — shape is plugin-defined.

SyncRunCurrent.title: SyncRunCurrent
SyncRunCurrent.description: In-flight sync run — populated when status IN ('queued','running','cancelling').

SyncScheduleBody.title: SyncScheduleBody
SyncScheduleBody.description:
Per-plugin schedule override.

B148: cron=None or "" clears the override (falls back to manifest).
B205 (v0.9.6): friendly form — supply interval_value + interval_unit
instead of a raw cron expression. The two forms are mutually exclusive
in a single request.

Examples:
    {"cron": "*/15 * * * *"}                      raw cron
    {"interval_value": 15, "interval_unit": "minutes"}  friendly form
    {"cron": null}                                 clear override

manifest_cron_display.title: Manifest Cron Display
manifest_cron_display.description: Human label for manifest_cron, when expressible.

scheduler_alive.title: Scheduler Alive
scheduler_alive.description: True iff the scheduler row was updated within the last 5 minutes.

SyncScheduleGetResponse.title: SyncScheduleGetResponse
SyncScheduleGetResponse.description: GET /api/plugins/{id}/sync-schedule — composite read used by the Settings tab.

SyncScheduleRegistry.title: SyncScheduleRegistry
SyncScheduleRegistry.description: sync_schedule_registry row — what the scheduler is actively tracking.

override_cron.title: Override Cron
override_cron.description: The newly-stored override, or null when clearing.

preview_next_fires.title: Preview Next Fires
preview_next_fires.description: Up to 5 ISO-8601 firing times from now under the new cron.

SyncScheduleSetResponse.title: SyncScheduleSetResponse
SyncScheduleSetResponse.description: POST /api/plugins/{id}/sync-schedule — write or clear an override.

cpu_load_pct_estimate.title: Cpu Load Pct Estimate
cpu_load_pct_estimate.description: (avg_duration_ms × runs_24h) / 86_400_000 × 100, capped at 100. % of one CPU continuously consumed by this sync.

SyncStatusResponse.title: SyncStatusResponse
SyncStatusResponse.description:
GET /api/plugins/{id}/sync/status — composite snapshot for the Sync card.

`current` is the in-flight run, or null when idle. `last_success` /
`last_failure` are the most recent terminal runs. `last_sync` mirrors
`last_success.completed_at` for backward compatibility with pre-v0.9.6
frontend code.

plugin.title: Plugin
plugin.description: Plugin slug, or null for host-owned tables

components.title: Components
components.description: Components now permitted to render after operator trust grant.

UninstallCheckDependent.title: UninstallCheckDependent
UninstallCheckDependent.description: Plugin that depends on the one being uninstalled.

dependents.title: Dependents
dependents.description: Other installed plugins that depend on this one — uninstalling without cascade is blocked.

references.title: References
references.description: External references to this plugin (fusions, dashboards, etc.).

tables.title: Tables
tables.description: DB tables that would be dropped if remove_data=true.

data_dirs.title: Data Dirs
data_dirs.description: Filesystem data dirs under data/{slug}/ (utility plugins).

tables_to_drop_if_data_removed.title: Tables To Drop If Data Removed
tables_to_drop_if_data_removed.description: Each Postgres table the plugin declares with its current size + row count. Drives the 'exactly what will be dropped' disclosure on the uninstall modal.

tables_to_drop_total_size_mb.title: Tables To Drop Total Size Mb

tables_to_drop_total_count.title: Tables To Drop Total Count
tables_to_drop_total_count.description: len(tables_to_drop_if_data_removed). Frontend uses this to decide whether to render the DELETE button at all.

UninstallCheckResponse.title: UninstallCheckResponse
UninstallCheckResponse.description: GET /api/plugins/{id}/uninstall-check — info for the confirmation modal.

UninstallCheckTable.title: UninstallCheckTable
UninstallCheckTable.description: Table that would be dropped if remove_data=true.

UninstallCheckTableWithSize.title: UninstallCheckTableWithSize
UninstallCheckTableWithSize.description:
B280 (v0.9.11.15): per-table data for the honest uninstall modal —
name + current row count + size on disk. Drives the "exactly what will
be dropped" disclosure block.

UninstallDependent.title: UninstallDependent
UninstallDependent.description: Single dependent plugin in the has_dependents response.

UpdateJobAlertSubscriptionBody.title: UpdateJobAlertSubscriptionBody
UpdateJobAlertSubscriptionBody.description: PUT /api/maintenance/job-alerts/{id}. Pass only the fields you're changing.

retention_days.title: Retention Days
retention_days.description: New retention threshold (0 means immediate purge of additional_where matches).

paused.title: Paused
paused.description: True to pause the policy; false to activate.

UpdateRetentionPolicyBody.title: UpdateRetentionPolicyBody
UpdateRetentionPolicyBody.description:
PUT /api/maintenance/retention/{policy_key} body.

Either field may be omitted; pass only what's changing.

UpdateStatus.title: UpdateStatus
UpdateStatus.description: plugin_update_status block attached to plugin entries (B144).

UserAnalyticsResponse.title: UserAnalyticsResponse
UserAnalyticsResponse.description:
GET /api/activity/analytics — admin analytics overview.

`devices`, `browsers`, `ip_activity`, `hourly_distribution` are
histogram-style maps keyed by the categorical value; treated as
open-ended dicts since the keys are inferred from user-agent / IP /
timestamp parsing.

UserSerialized.title: UserSerialized
UserSerialized.description:
Serialized user row — output of `_serialize()` in routes/auth.py.

`password_hash` is always stripped. `api_key` is truncated to first
8 chars + ellipsis. Datetimes are ISO-8601 strings.

Extra keys are allowed because user rows include columns added by
later migrations (e.g. `last_seen_at`, `color`) that may or may not
be present depending on schema state.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: Resolved permission set for this user's role.

UserWithPermissions.title: UserWithPermissions
UserWithPermissions.description: Per-user audit row in the matrix UI's Users tab.

VerifyResponse.title: VerifyResponse
VerifyResponse.description:
GET /api/auth/verify — token introspection.

Returns `{valid: false}` for any invalid/expired/missing token; the
caller-friendly fields are only set when the token resolves to an
active user.

sessionToken.name: X-Session-Token
sessionToken.description: Session token from `POST /api/auth/login`. Required for most endpoints.

apiKey.name: X-API-Key
apiKey.description: Programmatic API key. Alternative to session token for scripted automation.

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Login, sessions, password reset, API keys.

tags.name: plugins
tags.description: Install, uninstall, configure, list installed plugins.

tags.name: sync
tags.description: Manual sync triggers, schema setup, plugin health checks.

tags.name: jobs
tags.description: Job runs (sync + hooks). Fire-now, cancel, pause, resume.

tags.name: data-port
tags.description: Browse rows from any plugin table.
